parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: Mrs. Kate Smith
You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Now answer the questions on your screen. My name is Mrs Kate Smith. My husband's name is Robert. We have two children, both boys. The youngest is called John and he's eight. The eldest is called Simon and he's 14. We live at 9 Webley Avenue, Wesley.

Speaker 2:
Can you spell Webley, please?

Speaker 1: Mrs. Kate Smith
Sure. It's W-E-B-L-E-Y.

Speaker 2:
And do you have the postcode for that address?

Speaker 1: Mrs. Kate Smith
Oh, yes. It's WE46YS.

Speaker 2:
That's fine. Now, I assume, looking at the ages of your children, that you'll be looking at a primary institution for John and a secondary one for Simon. Is that right?

Speaker 1: Mrs. Kate Smith
Exactly.

Speaker 2:
Now, it's the middle of the school year, of course. Will you be wanting your children to go to their new schools right away?

Speaker 1: Mrs. Kate Smith
If possible, yes.

Speaker 2:
Fine. So, what is interesting is that I see on my computer that your address lies on the border of two catchment areas.

Speaker 1: Mrs. Kate Smith
Catchment areas? What are they?

Speaker 2:
Each school has a geographic area around it and, if you live within that area, you have the right to attend that school. If you do not live within the catchment area of a particular school, it can be much harder to get your child into that school.

Speaker 1: Mrs. Kate Smith
I see. Now, because you live on the border between two catchment areas, you have more choice. That's good. Let's look at the primary possibilities for John first.

Speaker 2:
There are two institutions that would be available for John. The first is Longmead School and the second is King's School. Do you have any information about them?

Speaker 1: Mrs. Kate Smith
Certainly.

Speaker 2:
Longmead School would be more convenient. From your address, it will take about 15 minutes to walk there. It's a mid-range school with around 150 boys and girls from kindergarten and then year 1 up to year 6.

Speaker 1: Mrs. Kate Smith
At 8 years old, John should be going into year 4. Is that right?

Speaker 2:
Yes, he's in year 4 right now.
